data.title: Robinhood launches new desktop platform, futures and index options
data.description:
At its HOOD Summit, Robinhood Markets (NASDAQ:HOOD) introduced a futures trading and index options on its mobile app and unveiled a new desktop trading platform designed for active traders.

The desktop platform, dubbed Robinhood Legend, allows users to set up custom layouts or choose from a group of layout templates, to trade with one click directly from their watchlist or from a chart, to have up to eight charts in one window, and offers real-time data, the company said.

The company also will launch futures trading in the Robinhood (HOOD) app, letting customers trade the S&P 500 (SPX), oil (CL1:COM), bitcoin (BTC-USD), and others.

It's also introducing index options. Like futures, index options offer tax advantages because they're subject to the 60/40 tax rule with 60% of gains taxed as long-term capital gains, the company said.

Robinhood Legend started to become available on Wednesday and will be available to all eligible customers in coming months.

Futures and index options will be rolled out in coming months. Initially, futures and index options will only be available on the Robinhood mobile app. Support on Robinhood Legend will follow, the company said.

Robinhood (HOOD) stock fell 1.5% in Thursday midday trading.

data.title: Kezar stock falls 11% after board rejects Concentra offer (update)
data.description:
Shares of Kezar Life Sciences (NASDAQ:KZR) fell 11% Thursday after the company said its board of directors had unanimously rejected an unsolicited takeover offer from Concentra Biosciences and adopted a stockholders rights plan.

Last week, Concentra offered to acquire all of Kezar’s (KZR) outstanding stock for $1.10 per share in cash, plus a contingent value right that represented the right to receive 80% of the net proceeds from the out-licensing or sale of Kezar's (KZR) programs or intellectual property.

Kezar (KZR) said Thursday that after reviewing the offer with its advisors, its board has determined the proposal “substantially undervalues” the company. It added that the offer implies an equity value for Kezar shareholders that is below the company’s available liquidity and fails to adequately value the potential of its drug candidate zetomipzomib, according to a statement.

Kezar added that in response to the offer and the “rapid” accumulation of 9.9% of Kezar’s stock by Concentra and its affiliates, it has immediately adopted a limited duration stockholders rights plan.

“Kezar continues to experience a significant and ongoing dislocation in the trading price of its common stock which does not reflect its fundamental value,” said Kezar Chairman Graham Cooper, in the statement. “We intend the rights plan to enable all of our stockholders to realize the long-term value of their investment.”

In connection with the shareholders rights plan, Kezar’s board also declared a dividend of one preferred share purchase right for each outstanding share of Kezar common stock, subject to certain restrictions. The dividend is payable to shareholders of record as of Oct. 28.

Kezar also announced on Thursday that it was discontinuing development of zetomipzomib for the treatment of lupus nephritis. The program was placed on clinical hold earlier this month due to safety concerns.

(Updates stock movement, adds news on zetomipzomib program.)

data.title: Zoom, Dynatrace upgraded on enterprise software strength: Wedbush
data.description:
Zoom (NASDAQ:ZM) and Dynatrace (NYSE:DT) were among enterprise software names upgraded by Wedbush on Thursday.

Zoom was upgraded to Outperform from Neutral. Its price target was pushed up to $85 from $80.

"Zoom's broad product portfolio allows it to address the market in multiple areas of communications and collaboration where the company sizes its addressable market growing to ~$235B by 2028 addressing Unified Communications, Contact Center, Webinars, Virtual Events and Sales Intelligence," Wedbush analysts, led by Daniel Ives, said in a note.

Dynatrace was also upgraded to Outperform from Neutral, and its price target was bumped to $67 from $55.

"A key difference between Dynatrace and other observability vendors is the focus on enterprise customers with greater than $1B in revenues with ~15,000 such organizations that it wants to target," Wedbush noted. "Dynatrace's hybrid model also makes it well suited to larger enterprises, which are more likely to have an on-prem infrastructure as well as some workloads in the cloud."

Dynatrace has added more than 100 net new logos in the past year as it focuses on higher-end clients.

Several other enterprise software firms with Outperform ratings had their price targets increased by Wedbush as well.

This includes CrowdStrike (NASDAQ:CRWD), which was increased to $330 from $315. MongoDB's (NASDAQ:MDB) price target was bumped to $320 from $280. SentinelOne's (NYSE:S) price target inched up to $32 from $30.

Zoom and Dynatrace edged up less than 1% by afternoon trading on Thursday.

  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Kezar discontinues development of zetomipzomib for lupus nephritis
data.description:
Kezar Life Sciences (NASDAQ:KZR) is discontinuing development of its drug zetomipzomib for the treatment of lupus nephritis so it can focus its resources on developing the product for autoimmune hepatitis, or AIH.

A Phase 2b study evaluating the drug for lupus nephritis was recently placed on clinical hold after four patients enrolled in the trial in the Philippines and Argentina died, including one who had been given a placebo.

The company added that the independent data monitoring committee overseeing a Phase 2a study of zetomipzomib for AIH has recommended the study continue without modification. Topline data is expected in the first half of 2025, according to a statement.

Kezar (KZR) said that as of Sept. 30, it had around $148M in cash and equivalents.

Kezar (KZR) also announced on Thursday that its board has rejected an unsolicited takeover proposal from Concentra Biosciences. The company has also adopted a shareholders rights plan, effective immediately.

Shares of Kezar (KZR) were down 11% in afternoon trading Thursday.

data.title: W.W. Grainger downgraded at Oppenheimer on valuation
data.description:
• W.W. Grainger (NYSE:GWW) late Wednesday was downgraded to Perform from a previous investment rating of Outperform by analysts at financial-services firm Oppenheimer. They said the provider of industrial goods is more fairly valued after a recent gain.
  • “We expect Grainger (GWW) to materially retain its accrued incremental P/E multiple in valuation over time, and our downgrade is not based on any changed view of fundamentals,” Christopher Glynn, analyst at Oppenheimer, said in an October 16 report.

data.title: Littelfuse upgraded at Oppenheimer on electrification outlook
data.description:
• Littelfuse (NASDAQ:LFUS) late Wednesday was upgraded to Outperform from a previous investment rating of Perform by analysts at financial-services firm Oppenheimer. They said the maker of automotive fuses and other electronics parts is poised for growth as the global economy becomes more electrified.
  • “Littelfuse’s (LFUS) leading global portfolio in circuit protection and power control should continue to benefit from electronification and electrification to support differentiated organic growth over time,” Christopher Glynn, analyst at Oppenheimer, said in an October 16 report.
  • Oppenheimer set a price target of $310 a share on Littelfuse (LFUS), based on an estimated price-to-earnings multiple of 27 times for 2025 earnings of $11.50 a share.

data.title: Phony bitcoin ETF post leads to arrest of alleged SEC X account hacker
data.description:
25-year-old Eric Council Jr. was arrested Thursday morning, and charged with hacking the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission's X account in January and posting a fake post regarding bitcoin (BTC-USD) exchange-traded funds, according to the Department of Justice.

On Jan. 9, the SEC's X account was compromised and a phony post claiming that the regulator had approved spot bitcoin (BTC-USD) ETFs briefly sent the price of bitcoin higher. The SEC regained control of its X account shortly thereafter, and the products were green lit on Jan. 10.

Now, the Federal Bureau of Investigations has arrested Council Jr. and is being charged by indictment with conspiracy to commit aggravated identity theft and access device fraud.

“The indictment alleges that Eric Council, Jr. unlawfully accessed the SEC’s account on X by using the stolen identity of a person who had access to the account to take over their cellphone number,” Principal Deputy Assistant Attorney General Argentieri said in a statement.

“Council, Jr.’s co-conspirators then allegedly used this unauthorized access to the X account to falsely announce that the SEC had approved listing Bitcoin ETFs, which caused the price of Bitcoin to rise by $1,000 and then fall by $2,000," he added.

The FBI Washington Field Office Criminal and Cyber Division is currently investigating the case, alongside the SEC-Office of Inspector General, the U.S. Attorney’s Office for the District of Columbia, and the Department of Justice’s Market Integrity and Major Frauds Unit and Computer Crime and Intellectual Property Section.

Bitcoin ETFs: (IBIT), (ARKB), (GBTC), (BRRR), (BTCO), (HODL), (BTCW), (FBTC), (BITB), (EZBC).

data.title: ProFrac plunges as Morgan Stanley cuts to Sell equivalent
data.description:
ProFrac (NASDAQ:ACDC) -8.2% in Thursday's trading after Morgan Stanley downgrades shares to Underweight from Equal Weight with a $6.50 price target, down from $7, seeing outsized risk in North America completions revisions risk and more attractive risk-reward opportunities elsewhere.

Morgan Stanley analyst Devin McDermott said he sees more downward revision risk for North America completions markets - ProFrac's core exposure - and the most risk in proppant markets where the company has outsized exposure, 25%-30% of long-term adjusted EBITDA vs. peers at 0-10%.

On relative performance drivers, ProFrac (ACDC) screens in-line on free cash flow revisions, free cash flow yield, and bear case leverage, and less attractive on EBITDA revisions, TSR yield, EV/EBITDA, PT upside, risk-reward, and volatility, McDermott says.

Beyond Q3, the analyst reiterates his preference for oilfield services stocks with international exposure over North America, including Baker Hughes (BKR) and SLB (SLB).
